hook: AI gave me a 47-step plan when I asked how to fix my morning routine. I needed 3.
caption:
AI gave me a 47-step plan when I asked how to fix my morning routine. I needed 3.

Here's what happened: I told ChatGPT my mornings felt chaotic. It came back with a full life overhaul. Sleep cycle optimization. Journaling frameworks. A hydration schedule. Cold plunge suggestions.

I closed the tab and made coffee.

The problem wasn't the AI. The problem was it didn't know I'm a 'just tell me the next move' person. I don't want the whole system. I want the smallest thing that makes today better than yesterday.

That's not a preference. That's a thinking style.

Some people genuinely need the full picture before they can act. They'd love that 47-step response. They'd build a spreadsheet around it.

But if you're wired to move first and adjust later — and the AI keeps handing you architecture when you need a door — that's not a you problem. That's a calibration problem.

BootFile figures out which type you are. Then every AI response you get is scoped to how your brain actually wants information.

Shorter. Sharper. Actually usable.

The 47-step plan people are out there and they deserve great AI too. This isn't about simple vs. complex. It's about matched vs. mismatched.

Which one are you?
